docs.title: About FindMyClient.org
docs.text: A focused micro-SaaS tool to help solo founders, freelancers, and small teams find and convert clients faster — without the bloat of enterprise platforms.

docs.title: What We Do
docs.text: FindMyClient.org is a lightweight, single-purpose tool that helps individuals and small businesses discover and manage potential clients efficiently. No bloated CRM, no enterprise sales calls — just a tool that does one thing well.

docs.title: Our Mission
docs.text: As a micro-SaaS, we believe client acquisition tools shouldn't be complex, expensive, or time-consuming. Our mission is to stay small, focused, and useful — solving one problem, finding clients, really well, instead of trying to be an all-in-one platform.

docs.title: Why We Exist
docs.text: Most platforms are bloated, over-engineered, and priced for teams much bigger than yours. FindMyClient.org is a micro-SaaS built to be fast, lightweight, practical, and results-focused, maintained by a small, independent team that actually uses what it builds.

docs.title: Core Principles
docs.text:
• Stay Small, Stay Focused

    We're not trying to become an everything-platform. One tool, one job, done well — that's the whole philosophy behind staying micro.

  • Simplicity Over Complexity

    Clean workflows and straightforward tools always win over feature-heavy dashboards that take weeks to learn.

  • Speed Over Unnecessary Features

    Performance and usability come before adding "just one more feature."

  • Transparency

    Clear, simple pricing with no hidden tiers, no enterprise sales calls, and no surprises.

  • Privacy-First Design

    We minimize unnecessary data collection and respect user privacy by default.

docs.title: Who It's For
docs.text:
FindMyClient.org is built for people who want a no-fuss tool rather than another platform to manage:

  • Freelancers

  • Solo Founders

  • Indie Agencies

  • Early-Stage Startups

  • Small Sales Teams

  • Developers

Anyone who wants to scale outreach without scaling overhead.

docs.title: Contact
docs.text:
Questions or feedback? As a small team, we read everything ourselves.

human@findmyclient.org

A Micro-SaaS Built for Practical Results

FindMyClient.org is an independent, focused micro-SaaS — built for performance, clarity, and practical workflows, not unnecessary complexity.

docs.title: API Tokens
docs.text:
API tokens allow you to authenticate requests to the FindMyClient API.

Once signed in, users can generate and manage API tokens directly from the dashboard.

docs.title: Getting Your API Token
docs.text:
Dashboard Access

API tokens are only available to authenticated users through the FindMyClient dashboard.

  1. Sign in to your FindMyClient account.
  2. Open the Dashboard.
  3. Navigate to the API Tokens section.
  4. Generate or copy your API token.

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
0.0.6

Include your API token in the request headers when making API calls.

token: YOUR_API_TOKEN

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text:
0.0.6 
    curl -X POST "https://findmyclient.org/api/search" \
    -H "token: YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -d '{
        "query": "singapore cafe"
    }'

docs.title: Security Recommendations
docs.text:
Keep Your Token Secure

Your API token provides access to your account and API usage.

  • Keep your API token private.
  • Do not expose tokens in frontend code or public repositories.
  • Rotate tokens regularly when needed.
  • Revoke compromised tokens immediately from the dashboard.

docs.title: Built for Automation
docs.text:
FindMyClient API tokens can be used with:

Use Case Primary Users Purpose Example AI Agents AI Developers, SaaS Builders Allow AI agents to autonomously discover and qualify prospects. Enrich company records before launching an outreach campaign. Internal Lead Generation Systems Organizations, Growth Teams Power proprietary lead generation platforms with fresh prospect data. A company builds its own lead dashboard using FindMyClient APIs. Workflow Automation Operations Teams, Agencies Automate lead discovery and data processing tasks. Automatically send newly discovered leads to a CRM or database. n8n Pipelines Automation Builders Integrate lead generation into no-code and low-code workflows. Trigger lead searches and route results to Google Sheets or Slack. Business Intelligence Tools Analysts, Researchers Use business data for reporting, market analysis, and decision-making. Analyze business density and market opportunities in a region. Developer Integrations Software Developers Embed lead generation and enrichment capabilities into applications. Build a custom prospecting platform using the FindMyClient API.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text: Latest platform and API updates for FindMyClient.

docs.title: v0.0.1 - 13 May 2026
docs.text:
Added:

Rate Limiting

- Added request rate limiting to protect the API from excessive usage.
- Implemented a global handler for HTTP `429 Too Many Requests`.
- Returns a structured JSON response when rate limits are exceeded.


Search Request Validation
Introduced a centralized validation layer for all search requests.

Input Sources Supported

- JSON request body
- Form data
- Query parameters

Validation Rules

- Query must be a string.
- Minimum length: 3 characters.
- Maximum length: 50 characters.
- Maximum word count: 8 words.

Security Improvements
Added multiple safeguards to improve request safety and prevent abuse.

- Blocks URL-based inputs (e.g. `http://`, `www.`)
- Prevents common injection patterns (SQL-like and script-based inputs)
- Detects repeated-character spam patterns
- Rejects inputs with excessive non-alphanumeric content ratio


Content Filtering
Introduced keyword-based filtering to block unsafe or irrelevant queries.

- Fraud-related terms
- Credential/password-related terms
- Gambling-related terms
- Adult content-related terms


Input Normalization

- Trims leading and trailing whitespace
- Collapses multiple spaces into a single space
- Normalizes query formatting before processing

docs.title: v0.0.2 - 24 May 2026
docs.text:
Added:

Schema Improvements (Machine Readable Results)

- Improved API response schema for consistent machine-readable output.
- Standardized response structure across all endpoints.
- Added explicit typing for key fields (e.g. query, results, metadata).
- Ensured predictable JSON keys to for agents and integrations.
- Added structured error objects with consistent error codes and messages.


API Usage Enforcement (Anti-Abuse Layer)

- Strengthened API usage controls to reduce spam and automated abuse.
- Added stricter request validation at gateway level.
- Improved rate-limit enforcement consistency across distributed requests.
- Added early request rejection to reduce backend load from invalid traffic.


Dashboard Improvements (Minor Fixes)

- Fixed UI inconsistency in dashboard data rendering.
- Improved alignment and spacing in key metric panels.
- Corrected minor display bug affecting recent request logs.
- Enhanced stability of real-time update feed under high request volume.

docs.title: Base URL
docs.text:
0.0.1 
https://findmyclient.org/api

docs.title: GET /result/leads/<job_id>
docs.text:
Retrieve enriched and processed leads generated from a completed job.

This endpoint provides validated, classified, and confidence-scored lead data derived from the original scraping output.

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text: GET /result/leads/654e0e93-1a14-44d3-97e1-d7fabaf782fd

docs.title: Client Workflow Integration
docs.text:
0.0.7

The client workflow automatically handles job completion and lead retrieval:

  • Poll /result/<job_id> until status is completed
  • Once completed, fetch enriched data from /result/leads/<job_id>
  • Downstream systems consume only the enriched lead dataset

This removes the need for manual post-processing of raw scraping results.

docs.title: Enriched Leads Response (Leads Endpoint)
Lead Object
docs.text: Field Type Description email string Extracted email address domain string Email domain email_type string Classification (personal, role, generic) confidence_score float Lead quality score (0–1) is_valid boolean Whether email passed validation source string Origin of the email (e.g. website_crawl)

docs.title: Summary Object
docs.text: Field Type Description summary.total_leads integer Total enriched leads summary.valid_leads integer Valid leads after filtering summary.role_based_filtered integer Role-based emails removed or downgraded

docs.title: Python Example
docs.text:
0.0.8 
import time
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://127.0.0.1:5000/api"
API_TOKEN = "YOUR-API-TOKEN"


def search(query: str):
    # 1. Start job
    res = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/search",
        params={
            "query": query,
            "token": API_TOKEN,
            "max_pages": 20,
            "max_websites": 20,
            "max_results": 20,
        },
        timeout=30,
    )
    res.raise_for_status()
    job_id = res.json()["job_id"]

    print("Job started:", job_id)

    # 2. Poll status
    while True:
        res = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/result/{job_id}", timeout=30)
        res.raise_for_status()
        data = res.json()

        print("Status:", data.get("status"))

        if data.get("status") == "completed":
            if data.get("error"):
                raise Exception(data["error"])
            break

        if data.get("status") == "failed":
            raise Exception(data.get("error", "Job failed"))

        # waiting for response to avoid rate-limit.
        time.sleep(30)  

    # 3. Get results
    res = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/result/leads/{job_id}", timeout=30)
    res.raise_for_status()

    return res.json()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    result = search("singapore cafe")

    for row in result.get("rows", []):
        print(row)

docs.title: Introduction
docs.text:
Welcome to the official documentation for the FindMyClient API.

The API is built for asynchronous search processing. Each search request is queued as a background job and returns a unique job_id. You can retrieve results by polling the result endpoint until the job status is marked as completed.

Both GET and POST methods are supported for flexible integration with applications, scripts, AI agents, and automation workflows.

Async Processing

Search requests are processed in the background to support scalable business discovery and large web data operations.

docs.title: Workflow Overview
docs.text:
0.0.1

The API follows a simple asynchronous workflow.

docs.title: Submit Request
docs.text:
0.0.1

Call the /search endpoint to create a new background search job.

POST /search

docs.title: Receive Job ID
Check Status
Completion

Enrich company records before launching an outreach campaign. Internal Lead Generation Systems Organizations, Growth Teams Power proprietary lead generation platforms with fresh prospect data. A company builds its own lead dashboard using FindMyClient APIs. Workflow Automation Operations Teams, Agencies Automate lead discovery and data processing tasks. Trigger lead searches and route results to Google Sheets or Slack. Business Intelligence Tools Analysts, Researchers Use business data for reporting, market analysis, and decision-making. Build a custom prospecting platform using the FindMyClient API.

docs.title: Limitations
docs.text: The FindMyClient API has the following limitations to ensure stable, fair, and reliable usage for all users.

docs.title: Rate Limits
Asynchronous Processing
docs.text:
0.0.1

All requests are processed asynchronously.

After submitting a request, you must poll the result endpoint to retrieve the final output:

/result/{job_id}

docs.title: Processing Time
docs.text:
0.0.1

Processing time may vary depending on:

  • Query complexity
  • Current system load
  • Availability of external data sources

Some requests may complete quickly, while others may take longer depending on workload and crawling depth.

docs.title: Data Availability
docs.text:
0.0.1

Results depend on publicly available data. In some cases:

  • Business contact information may not exist
  • Email addresses may not be publicly accessible
  • Partial or incomplete results may be returned

docs.title: Concurrent Requests
docs.text:
0.0.1

Concurrent request volume is limited to ensure system stability.

High parallel usage may result in:

  • Request throttling
  • Increased latency
  • Delayed job processing

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
0.0.1

To ensure optimal performance:

  • Use controlled request intervals
  • Avoid unnecessary parallel requests
  • Cache results when possible
  • Poll results with reasonable intervals (e.g., 10–60 seconds)

docs.title: n8n Integration
docs.text:
Connect FindMyClient.org with n8n using the built-in HTTP Request node.

This integration is lightweight, flexible, and does not require a custom n8n community node. Since the FindMyClient API is REST-based, n8n can interact with it directly using standard HTTP requests.

docs.title: Workflow Overview
docs.text:
0.0.6

The workflow:

  1. Accepts a search query.
  2. Starts a FindMyClient search job.
  3. Retrieves the generated job_id.
  4. Polls the API until the search is completed.
  5. Extracts discovered emails.
  6. Splits emails into individual records.
  7. Optionally:
  8. Save results to Google Sheets
  9. Send emails via Gmail

Try n8n Workflow

Get the latest automation workflow below.

⬇ Try n8n Workflow now

View and import the workflow directly from the official n8n template page.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
0.0.6

Before using this workflow:

  • n8n installed
  • FindMyClient API token
  • Optional Google Sheets credentials
  • Optional Gmail credentials

docs.title: Configure Search Query
docs.text:
0.0.6

Add a Edit Fields (Set) node and input the search keyword.

docs.title: Start Search Job
Store Job ID
docs.text:
0.0.6

Add a Edit Fields (Set) to store job_id.

This ID is used for polling search progress.

docs.title: Poll Search Status
Processing
docs.text:
{
  "status": "processing"
}

docs.title: Completed
docs.text:
{
  "status": "completed",
  "result": {
    "output": {
      "emails": [
        "info@example.com",
        "sales@example.com"
      ]
    }
  }
}

docs.title: Wait and Retry
docs.text:
0.0.6

use Wait node and set the parameters.

If the status is not:

completed

The workflow:

  1. Waits 30 seconds
  2. Reuses the existing job_id
  3. Checks the API again

This creates a polling loop until the search finishes.

docs.title: Extract Results
Split Emails
Optional: Save to Google Sheets
docs.text:
The workflow includes a disabled Google Sheets node.

Example spreadsheet:

Email info@example.com sales@example.com contact@example.com

Enable the node and configure your spreadsheet credentials.

docs.title: Optional: Send Email Notifications
docs.text:
The workflow includes a disabled Gmail node.

Possible use cases:

  • Notify sales teams
  • Send discovered leads
  • Trigger CRM imports
  • Alert automation systems

Enable the Gmail node and configure OAuth credentials.

0.0.6

    "output": {

0.0.6

Replace the Set node with:

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
Common scenarios:

Scenario Action Search still processing Wait and retry Empty result set End workflow Invalid API token Update credentials API timeout Retry request Network failure Use n8n retry logic 0.0.6

docs.title: Example End-to-End Flow
docs.text:
Input:

{
  "query": "italy winery"
}

Output:

[
  "info@winery1.com",
  "sales@winery2.com",
  "contact@winery3.com"
]

The workflow automatically handles:

  • Job creation
  • Polling
  • Status checking
  • Email extraction
  • Record splitting
  • Export and notification steps

making it suitable for automated lead generation pipelines.

docs.title: Search Parameters
docs.text:
0.0.8

The /search endpoint accepts the following parameters, either as query parameters or as a JSON body.

Parameter Type Required Description query string Yes The search query used to find relevant websites and content. token string Yes Your API authentication token. max_pages integer No Maximum number of pages to crawl per website. Higher values allow deeper crawling at additional cost. max_websites integer No Maximum number of websites to include in the search. Costs scale with the number of websites beyond the included tier threshold. max_results integer No Maximum number of results to return in the response.

docs.title: Python Example
docs.text:
0.0.8 
import requests

BASE_URL = "https://api.example.com"
API_TOKEN = "YOUR-API-TOKEN"
query = "Italy Winery"

response = requests.post(
    f"{BASE_URL}/search",
    params={
        "query": query,
        "token": API_TOKEN,
        "max_pages": 20,
        "max_websites": 20,
        "max_results": 20,
    },
    timeout=30,
)

Notes

  • Omitted parameters fall back to default values defined by your account tier.
  • Increasing max_websites beyond the included threshold incurs additional cost per extra website.
  • timeout is a client-side setting and is not sent to the API.

docs.title: Privacy Policy
docs.text: Last updated: Jun 12, 2026

docs.title: 1.  Data Controller
docs.text:
This website is operated by FindMyClient.org ("we," "us," or "our"), which acts as the data controller for personal data processed through this service in accordance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and other applicable data protection laws.

For any questions regarding this policy or our data practices, you may contact us at support@findmyclient.org.

docs.title: 2.  Data We Collect
docs.text:
We collect only the data necessary to operate, secure, and improve the service.

Account Data includes your email address and authentication information used to create and manage your account.

Usage Data includes information about the pages you visit and the actions you perform within the platform, which helps us understand how the service is used and where it can be improved.

Technical Data includes your IP address, browser type, and device information, collected automatically to ensure compatibility, security, and performance.

Billing Data includes payment and subscription information, which is processed and stored by third-party payment providers rather than directly by us.

docs.title: 3.  Legal Basis for Processing
docs.text: We process personal data on the basis of a contract, where processing is necessary to provide access to the service and fulfill our obligations to you. We also rely on legitimate interest where processing helps us maintain, secure, and improve the platform, including preventing abuse and fraud. In certain cases, processing is necessary to comply with legal obligations, such as tax, accounting, and other regulatory requirements.

docs.title: 4.  How We Use Data
docs.text: The data we collect is used to provide and maintain the platform, process subscriptions and payments, improve functionality and the overall user experience, and detect and prevent fraud, abuse, and security issues. We do not use personal data for purposes incompatible with those described in this policy.

docs.title: 5.  Data Sharing
docs.text: We do not sell personal data to third parties. In order to operate the service, limited data may be shared with trusted third-party service providers acting on our behalf, such as hosting providers, analytics providers, and payment processors. These providers are contractually obligated to process data only for the purposes specified and in accordance with applicable data protection laws.

docs.title: 6.  Data Retention
docs.text: Personal data is retained only for as long as necessary to fulfill the purposes described in this policy, or as required to comply with legal, accounting, or regulatory obligations. Once these purposes no longer apply, data is securely deleted or anonymized.

docs.title: 7.  Your GDPR Rights
docs.text: If you are located in the European Economic Area, you have certain rights regarding your personal data under the GDPR. These include the right to access the personal data we hold about you, request correction or deletion of that data, restrict or object to certain processing activities, request portability of your data to another provider, and withdraw consent at any time where processing is based on consent. To exercise any of these rights, please contact us using the details provided in this policy.

docs.title: 8.  Security
docs.text: We implement appropriate technical and organizational measures designed to protect personal data against unauthorized access, alteration, disclosure, or destruction. While we take reasonable steps to safeguard your information, no method of transmission or storage is completely secure, and we cannot guarantee absolute security.

docs.title: 9.  International Transfers
docs.text: Where personal data is transferred outside the European Economic Area, we ensure that appropriate safeguards are in place, such as Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) or other legally recognized mechanisms providing an equivalent level of data protection.

docs.title: 10.  Cookies
docs.text: We use essential cookies required for authentication, session management, and core platform functionality. These cookies are necessary for the service to operate and cannot be disabled. Where required by law, optional analytics cookies may be used only with your consent.

docs.title: 11.  Contact
docs.text: If you have questions about this Privacy Policy or wish to exercise your data protection rights, please contact us at human@findmyclient.org.

docs.title: Resources
docs.text:
FindMyClient.org provides a real-time API for discovering public business contact information.

A community open-source version is available for local use, while the hosted API delivers fast, scalable, up-to-date results for developers and automation workflows.

Github Repo: 

https://github.com/Rottie420/findmyclient

docs.title: Base URL
docs.text:
0.0.1 

https://findmyclient.org/api

docs.title: GET /result/<job_id>
docs.text: Retrieve the current status and results of a search job.

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text: GET /result/654e0e93-1a14-44d3-97e1-d7fabaf782fd

docs.title: Processing Response
docs.text:
0.0.6

Returned while the job is still running.

{
  "created_at": "Sun, 24 May 2026 00:00:46 GMT",
  "error": null,
  "result": null,
  "status": "processing",
  "user": "user@test.com"
}

docs.title: Response Fields (Processing)
docs.text: 0.0.6 Field Type Description created_at string (timestamp) When the job was created (UTC) error string | null Execution errors, or null if none result object | null Empty until job completes status string Current job state (e.g. processing) user string (email) Account that initiated the request

docs.title: Completed Response
Job Metadata
docs.text: Field Type Description created_at string (timestamp) Job creation time (UTC) credit_cost integer API credits used error string | null Global job error (if any) status string Job state (e.g. completed)

docs.title: Result Object
docs.text: Field Type Description result object Root container for extracted data

docs.title: Errors
docs.text: Field Type Description result.errors array Non-fatal crawl errors result.errors[].code string Error identifier (e.g. crawl_failed) result.errors[].count integer Occurrence count result.errors[].retryable boolean Whether retry is possible

docs.title: Input
docs.text: Field Type Description result.input.query string Original search query

docs.title: Metadata
docs.text: Field Type Description result.meta.agent string Scraper version used result.meta.execution_time_seconds float Processing time result.meta.timestamp string Completion timestamp

docs.title: Output Data
docs.text: Field Type Description result.output.emails array Extracted email list result.output.total_emails_found integer Unique emails found result.output.total_failed_websites integer Failed website crawls result.output.total_websites_scanned integer Total scanned domains

docs.title: Status Flags
docs.text: Field Type Description result.status.partial_failure boolean Some sources failed but job completed result.status.success boolean Overall success state

docs.title: Base URL
docs.text:
0.0.1 

https://findmyclient.org/api

docs.title: POST / GET /search
docs.text:
Submit a new search request.

AI Agent Ready

The API returns structured JSON responses optimized for AI agents, automation systems, CRMs, and developer workflows.

docs.title: Endpoint
docs.text: POST /search

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text:
{
  "query": "singapore cafe"
}

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text:
curl -X POST "https://findmyclient.org/api/search" \
  -H "token: YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "query": "singapore cafe"
  }'

docs.title: GET Request
docs.text:
0.0.6 
GET /search?query=singapore+cafe&token=YOUR-API-TOKEN

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text: curl "https://findmyclient.org/api/search?query=singapore+cafe&token=YOUR-API-TOKEN"

docs.title: Search Response
Response Fields
docs.text:
0.0.6

The following table describes the properties returned in the API response body.

Field Type Description error string Details regarding any syntax or processing errors encountered. status string CuThe current execution state of the requested job. job_id string Unique identifier assigned to the specific search job. credits_remaining int The remaining balance of API credits available to the account. user string The username or identifier associated with the active account.

docs.title: Basic Usage
docs.text:
This example demonstrates how to interact with the FindMyClient API using Python.

It covers the full asynchronous workflow:

  • Create a search job
  • Poll for completion
  • Retrieve final results

Info

This approach is ideal for automation scripts, backend systems, and AI agent integrations.

docs.title: Python Example
docs.text:
0.0.6 
import time
import requests

BASE_URL = "https://findmyclient.org/api"
API_TOKEN = "YOUR-API-TOKEN"


def search(query: str):
    # Start search job
    response = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/search",
        params={"query": query, "token": API_TOKEN},
        timeout=30
    )

    response.raise_for_status()
    data = response.json()

    job_id = data["job_id"]
    print(f"Job started: {job_id}")

    # Poll for results
    while True:
        result = requests.get(
            f"{BASE_URL}/result/{job_id}",
            timeout=30
        )

        result.raise_for_status()
        payload = result.json()
        status = payload["status"]

        if status == "completed":
            if payload.get("error"):
                raise Exception(payload["error"])

            return payload["result"]

        print("Still processing...")
        time.sleep(60)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    output = search("singapore cafe")
    print("\nFinal Result:\n")
    print(output)

docs.title: Start a Search Job
docs.text:
0.0.6

Start a new search job by sending a query to the API. The request is processed asynchronously and returns a job_id that can be used to retrieve results later.

bashpythonshell 
curl -X POST "https://findmyclient.org/api/search" \
  -H "token: YOUR_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "query": "singapore cafe"
  }'

import requests

response = requests.post(
    "https://findmyclient.org/api/search",
    headers={"token": "YOUR_API_TOKEN"},
    json={"query": "singapore cafe"}
)

response.json()

Invoke-RestMethod -Method POST `
  -Uri "https://findmyclient.org/api/search" `
  -Headers @{token="YOUR_API_TOKEN"} `
  -Body (@{query="singapore cafe"} | ConvertTo-Json)

docs.title: Get Results
docs.text:
0.0.6

Retrieve the status and results of a previously submitted search job using its job_id. Once processing is complete, the API returns structured company and contact data in a machine-readable JSON format.

bashpythonshell 
curl -X GET "https://findmyclient.org/api/result/YOUR_JOB_ID"

import requests

response = requests.get(
    "https://findmyclient.org/api/result/YOUR_JOB_ID",
)

response.json()

Invoke-RestMethod -Method GET `
  -Uri "https://findmyclient.org/api/result/YOUR_JOB_ID"

docs.title: Get Leads
docs.text:
0.0.7

Retrieve enriched lead results for a completed scraping job using its job_id. This endpoint returns structured lead data including business details, verified emails, confidence scores, and metadata from the enrichment pipeline.

bashpythonshell 
curl -X GET "https://findmyclient.org/api/result/leads/654e0e93-1a14-44d3-97e1-d7fabaf782fd"

import requests

job_id = "654e0e93-1a14-44d3-97e1-d7fabaf782fd"

response = requests.get(
    f"https://findmyclient.org/api/result/leads/{job_id}",
)

print(response.json())

Invoke-RestMethod -Method GET `
  -Uri "https://findmyclient.org/api/result/leads/654e0e93-1a14-44d3-97e1-d7fabaf782fd"

Tips

Search jobs are processed asynchronously. Save the returned job_id and poll the result endpoint until the status becomes completed.

docs.title: Releases
docs.text:
0.0.1 - 13 May 2026

Initial release improvements introducing rate limiting, validation, and security hardening for the FindMyClient API.

docs.title: Added Rate Limiting
docs.text:
• Introduced request rate limiting to protect the API from excessive usage
  • Implemented a global handler for HTTP 429 Too Many Requests
  • Returns a structured JSON response when rate limits are exceeded

docs.title: Search Request Validation
docs.text: A centralized validation layer was introduced for all search requests.

docs.title: Supported Input Sources
docs.text:
• JSON request body
  • Form data
  • Query parameters

docs.title: Validation Rules
docs.text:
• Query must be a string
  • Minimum length: 3 characters
  • Maximum length: 50 characters
  • Maximum word count: 8 words

docs.title: Security Improvements
docs.text:
Multiple safeguards were added to improve request safety and prevent abuse:

  • Blocks URL-based inputs (e.g. http://, www.)
  • Prevents common injection patterns (SQL-like and script-based inputs)
  • Detects repeated-character spam patterns
  • Rejects inputs with excessive non-alphanumeric content ratio

docs.title: Content Filtering
docs.text:
Keyword-based filtering was introduced to block unsafe or irrelevant queries:

  • Fraud-related terms
  • Credential/password-related terms
  • Gambling-related terms
  • Adult content-related terms

docs.title: Input Normalization
docs.text:
All incoming queries are normalized before processing:

  • Trims leading and trailing whitespace
  • Collapses multiple spaces into a single space
  • Standardizes query formatting for consistent processing

docs.title: Schema Improvements (Machine Readable Results)
docs.text:
Improved API response consistency for AI agents, automation systems, and developer integrations.

  • Standardized response structures across all endpoints
  • Improved machine-readable JSON formatting
  • Added explicit typing for key fields such as queries, metadata, and results
  • Ensured predictable JSON keys for agents and integrations
  • Added structured error objects with consistent error codes and messages

docs.title: API Usage Enforcement (Anti-Abuse Layer)
docs.text:
Strengthened API protection and abuse prevention systems.

  • Added stricter request validation at the gateway layer
  • Improved distributed rate-limit enforcement consistency
  • Added early request rejection for invalid or abusive traffic
  • Reduced unnecessary backend processing load from malformed requests

docs.title: Dashboard Improvements (Minor Fixes)
docs.text:
Several dashboard stability and UI improvements were introduced.

  • Fixed UI inconsistencies in dashboard data rendering
  • Improved spacing and alignment in metric panels
  • Corrected display issues affecting recent request logs
  • Enhanced stability of the real-time activity feed under high request volume

docs.title: Performance & Reliability Improvements
docs.text:
Significant improvements focused on system stability, request success rates, and overall production performance across Find My Client.

  • Introduced a validated proxy pool system with automatic scoring, rotation, and failure tracking to improve request reliability
  • Improved fallback handling to ensure consistent results when primary data sources fail
  • Enhanced email discovery reliability through more resilient routing and execution paths
  • Reduced failure rates under concurrent load and improved request success consistency
  • Strengthened production stability for long-running and high-volume operations

docs.title: Observability & System Visibility
docs.text:
Improved visibility into system behavior for better monitoring, debugging, and operational control.

  • Standardized logging structure across core services for consistent output formatting
  • Improved debug signal clarity for production troubleshooting
  • Enhanced traceability of request execution paths and failure states

docs.title: Data Integrity & Validation
docs.text:
Improved internal data handling to ensure more reliable and predictable outputs.

  • Strengthened structured data validation using typed models for consistency across processing layers
  • Improved handling of malformed or incomplete data responses
  • Increased overall reliability of downstream processing pipelines

docs.title: Platform Stability Improvements
docs.text:
Focused on reducing instability in production environments and improving execution consistency.

  • Improved concurrency handling under load to reduce race conditions and failures
  • Enhanced CI/CD execution control to ensure cleaner, version-controlled deployments
  • Improved containerized deployment stability and runtime consistency
  • Reduced request timeouts and improved long-running process reliability

docs.title: Cost Optimization & Efficiency
docs.text:
This release introduces multiple backend optimizations designed to reduce operational costs while maintaining data quality and processing performance.

  • Reduced infrastructure and API operating costs
  • Eliminated unnecessary requests during large-scale data collection
  • Improved request efficiency to maximize throughput with fewer external API calls
  • Reduced infrastructure overhead during high-volume processing operations

docs.title: Reliability & Processing Improvements
docs.text:
Focused on improving execution reliability, fault tolerance, and long-running job stability.

  • Improved background processing resilience across concurrent workloads
  • Added safeguards to prevent stalled or unresponsive processing tasks
  • Enhanced handling of partial failures so successful operations continue uninterrupted
  • Improved stability when processing large datasets and extended scraping sessions
  • Strengthened overall execution consistency across production environments

docs.title: Communication Infrastructure
docs.text:
Enhanced outbound communication reliability through improvements to the email delivery stack.

  • Migrated to a new email delivery provider for improved message reliability
  • Improved email infrastructure stability and delivery consistency
  • Enhanced support for production-scale outbound communications

docs.title: Monitoring & Visibility
docs.text:
Improved system observability to provide better operational awareness and troubleshooting capabilities.

  • Enhanced warning and alert visibility across processing pipelines
  • Improved monitoring of execution states and background operations
  • Strengthened logging consistency for easier diagnostics and issue tracking

docs.title: Platform Stability & Code Quality
docs.text:
Additional improvements focused on maintainability, scalability, and long-term platform reliability.

  • Simplified internal processing workflows for improved maintainability
  • Refined execution flow to improve scalability and future extensibility
  • Improved internal data handling consistency across processing layers
  • Cleaned up code structure and naming conventions for easier maintenance
  • Resolved multiple edge cases that could interrupt processing workflows

docs.title: Lead Enrichment Pipeline & Email Intelligence Improvements
docs.text:
This release introduces a full lead enrichment pipeline that transforms completed scraping jobs into structured, verified, and confidence-scored leads. It also improves email classification accuracy by refining how role-based and generic emails are filtered out before identifying personal contacts.

  • Added a complete lead enrichment pipeline for processing finished scraping jobs into structured lead data
  • Introduced email verification using domain validation and MX record checks to improve deliverability confidence
  • Implemented confidence scoring for leads based on email validity, domain quality, and classification signals
  • Improved email classification logic to better distinguish personal contacts from role-based and generic addresses
  • Enhanced filtering of blocked prefixes and domains to reduce false-positive lead identification

docs.title: New Lead Retrieval Endpoint & Workflow Integration
docs.text:
A new API layer has been introduced to expose enriched lead data directly from completed jobs.

  • Added /leads/<job_id> endpoint to serve enriched and processed leads
  • Added load_job_result helper to simplify access to completed job data
  • Updated client workflow to automatically poll /result and fetch enriched leads from /result/leads
  • Streamlined job-to-lead retrieval flow for faster downstream consumption

docs.title: Data Quality & Email Filtering Improvements
docs.text:
Focused improvements on lead quality, ensuring cleaner and more actionable outputs.

  • Added stricter filtering for role-based email prefixes (e.g. info, support, sales)
  • Introduced generic domain filtering to reduce low-quality or non-personal leads
  • Ensured proper classification of valid personal-name emails for higher accuracy
  • Reduced false positives in lead generation through improved validation logic

docs.title: Refactors & Pipeline Centralization
docs.text:
Improved internal structure to make lead generation more maintainable and reusable across services.

  • Centralized lead-building logic into a dedicated service module
  • Refactored configuration-driven filtering for reusable prefix and domain rules
  • Streamlined internal data flow between scraping, processing, and lead generation layers
  • Improved separation between job execution and lead enrichment responsibilities

docs.title: Dynamic Tier-Based Search Pricing
docs.text:
This release introduces dynamic, tier-based billing for searches and replaces ad-hoc request parsing with validated schemas, alongside a configuration cleanup pass.

  • Replaced static search pricing with a dynamic cost model based on request size
  • Applied an incremental cost increase for searches exceeding a defined website threshold
  • Integrated dynamic cost calculation directly into the search endpoint, deducting credits accordingly
  • Added new parameters to control crawl depth, result limits, and website source caps

docs.title: Request Handling Fixes
docs.text:
Improvements to ensure search requests are handled more reliably across different client patterns.

  • Fixed errors on POST requests sent without a JSON body
  • Allowed POST requests to rely on query parameters instead of JSON bodies, preserving backward compatibility

docs.title: Validation & Configuration Refactors
docs.text:
Internal improvements to request validation and configuration management.

  • Introduced a validated request schema for normalized handling of JSON bodies and query parameters
  • Replaced manual parsing and type casting with schema-based validation, simplifying tier limit enforcement
  • Improved error handling with structured validation responses
  • Removed hardcoded constants from configuration for improved maintainability
